mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand that drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: RSAFS rises but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% indicates labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE >5.0% but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate, real interest rates are negative, incentivizing equity investment over bonds and producing positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between real rate signal and 20-day forward returns is below 0.05 or negative over any 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recoveries, rising industrial production signals expanding capacity and improving earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises but SPX 60-day returns are negative in over half of early-cycle instances identified by NBER dating.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) by more than 10% month-over-month signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After HOUST drops >10% m/m, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in the federal funds rate over the past 3 months signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS rises but SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and rise in the unemployment rate over two consecutive months signals a severe labor market deterioration that strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises in same month
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, SPX 60-day returns are negative in less than 60% of cases or odds ratio versus baseline is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime (CPI YoY above 3%), rising retail sales signal nominal demand that adds to inflationary pressure, prompting tighter monetary policy and lowering S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: In high-inflation months, RSAFS increase is followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year window.
